output:
## Part 1 — Atomic Decomposition

**Deliverables**
- Brand audit (current state diagnostic)
- Competitive landscape map
- Positioning statement
- Messaging architecture (value props, proof points, audience messaging)
- Voice & tone guide
- Naming recommendation
- Tagline / verbal identity
- Visual direction brief (mood, not execution)
- Brand guide PDF (final compiled doc)
- Internal rollout deck (for team alignment)
- Agency/designer handoff package

**Time blocks**
- 60-min kickoff call
- 45-min stakeholder interview (per interview)
- 60-min customer interview (per interview)
- 90-min positioning workshop
- 60-min messaging workshop
- 60-min mid-engagement review
- 90-min final presentation
- 30-min post-delivery debrief

**Access tiers**
- Async-only (email/Loom, 48-hr response)
- Standard (scheduled calls + async, 24-hr response)
- Priority (Slack/text, same-day response)

**Support levels**
- Zero revisions (one-shot deliverable)
- 1 revision round per deliverable
- Unlimited revisions within engagement window
- 30-day post-delivery office hours
- 90-day post-delivery office hours

**Customizations**
- # of stakeholder interviews (3 / 6 / 10)
- # of customer interviews (0 / 3 / 6)
- B2B vs. B2C vs. hybrid
- Internal-only vs. agency-handoff-ready output
- Single-brand vs. multi-brand / sub-brand architecture
- Industry depth (generalist read vs. deep category immersion)

---

## Part 2 — Configuration Architecture

| Dimension | What it controls | Levels | Dependencies |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Depth** | Research thoroughness — interviews, audit rigor, category immersion | Light (3 stakeholder, no customer) / Standard (6 stakeholder, 3 customer) / Deep (10 stakeholder, 6 customer, category immersion) | Sprint pace blocked at Deep; Customer interviews require min Standard |
| **Breadth** | Brand-system scope | Positioning-only / Positioning + Messaging / Full verbal system (+ Voice + Naming/Tagline) | Naming requires Full verbal; Sub-brand architecture requires Full verbal |
| **Pace** | Calendar duration | Sprint (2 weeks) / Standard (6 weeks) / Extended (10 weeks) | Sprint blocked with Deep depth; Sprint blocked with customer interviews |
| **Format** | Output packaging | Working doc only / Brand guide PDF / Brand guide + Rollout deck + Handoff package | Handoff package requires min Positioning + Messaging breadth |
| **Support** | Access + revisions | Async-only, 1 revision / Standard access, 2 revisions / Priority access, unlimited revisions + 90-day office hours | Priority access requires min Standard pace |
| **Architecture Scope** *(domain-specific)* | # of brands/sub-brands handled | Single brand / Master + 1 sub-brand / Full house-of-brands (3+) | Multi-brand requires Deep depth and Extended pace |

**Invalid combinations the configurator must block:**
- Sprint pace + Deep depth
- Sprint pace + customer interviews
- Naming/tagline without Full verbal breadth
- Handoff package with Positioning-only breadth
- Priority access on Sprint pace
- Multi-brand architecture on anything less than Deep + Extended

---

## Part 3 — Three Tiers + Build-Your-Own

| Tier | Configuration | Positioning Line | Pricing Logic |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Lite — Positioning Sprint** | Light depth · Positioning-only · Sprint (2w) · Working doc · Async support · Single brand | For founders who know they have a positioning problem and need a senior strategist's answer fast. | **$6,500** — ~43% of flagship. Anchored against the cost of continuing to launch with bad positioning; entry price for senior-strategist work (junior strategists charge $3–5K for similar). |
| **Core — Brand Strategy Engagement** *(flagship)* | Standard depth · Positioning + Messaging · Standard (6w) · Brand guide PDF · Standard support · Single brand | For funded startups and growing companies who need a complete verbal brand foundation before scaling marketing or hiring an agency. | **$15,000** (existing price held). Anchored as senior-solo work at ~⅓ of agency engagements ($40–60K at Red Antler / Lexicon tier); flagship discount vs. BYO-summed equivalent (~$18K). |
| **Premium — Full Brand System + Embed** | Deep depth · Full verbal system · Extended (10w) · Brand guide + Rollout deck + Handoff · Priority support · up to Master + 1 sub-brand | For Series A+ companies and rebrands with internal teams that need naming, sub-brand logic, and a strategist embedded through agency handoff. | **$32,000** — sits below consultancy retainers ($45–75K) and below sum-of-parts BYO (~$38K). Anchored against the cost of a bad agency handoff or a naming agency engagement alone ($25–40K). |

### Build-Your-Own Framework

**Base:** $6,500 (Lite configuration)

**Per-dimension upgrades:**
- Depth: Light → Standard +$3,500 · Standard → Deep +$5,500
- Breadth: +Messaging +$2,500 · +Full verbal (Voice + Naming) +$4,500
- Pace: Sprint → Standard +$1,000 · Standard → Extended +$2,500
- Format: +Brand guide PDF +$1,500 · +Rollout deck & Handoff +$3,000
- Support: Async → Standard +$1,500 · Standard → Priority +$3,500
- Architecture: +1 sub-brand +$6,000 · House-of-brands +$14,000

**Floor:** $6,500 (Lite minimum — anything below is a coaching call, not a productized engagement)

**Ceiling:** $28,000 — above this, auto-route to Premium flagship (better value) or scope as fully custom

**Configurator-enforced dependencies:** see invalid combinations in Part 2

**Flagship-protection check:**
- BYO to reach Core config: $6,500 + 3,500 + 2,500 + 1,000 + 1,500 + 1,500 = **$16,500** vs. Core flagship $15,000 ✓
- BYO to reach Premium config: $6,500 + 5,500 + 4,500 + 2,500 + 3,000 + 3,500 + 6,000 = **$31,500** vs. Premium flagship $32,000 — *adjust: drop Premium to $29,500 OR raise sub-brand upgrade to +$8,000.* Recommend raising sub-brand to +$8,000 (it's the highest-leverage upgrade and currently underpriced relative to the work).

---

Want me to **stress-test edge cases** (e.g., "B2B SaaS rebrand with two sub-brands and a 3-week deadline" — does the configurator route correctly?), or re-emit Parts 2 and 3 as **JSON/YAML for a Typeform or Webflow configurator**?

*Assuming online/remote fitness coaching for general adults (not elite athletes, not in-person personal training); will re-anchor with $ figures once you name the niche (e.g., postpartum strength, masters runners, hybrid athletes, fat-loss for execs) and your current 1-on-1 monthly rate.*

## Part 1 — Atomic Decomposition

**Deliverables**
- Custom training program (written, periodized)
- Nutrition plan / macro targets
- Habit-tracking template
- Progress-photo / measurement protocol
- Form-check video review (per submission)
- Monthly progress report

**Time blocks**
- 60-min onboarding/intake call
- 30-min monthly check-in call
- 15-min weekly check-in call
- Live-coached training session (per session)

**Access tiers**
- Email-only (48hr response)
- In-app messaging (24hr response)
- Direct text/WhatsApp (same-day)
- Voxer/async voice (same-day)

**Support levels**
- Program updates: monthly / bi-weekly / weekly / on-demand
- Form-check submissions: 0 / 2 per month / unlimited
- Nutrition adjustments: monthly / weekly
- Off-hours emergency access: yes/no

**Customizations**
- Training goal (strength / hypertrophy / fat loss / endurance / hybrid)
- Equipment context (full gym / home gym / minimal / bodyweight)
- Training frequency (2 / 3 / 4 / 5+ days)
- Nutrition depth (none / guidelines / full macros / meal plan)
- Mobility/recovery programming (on/off)

## Part 2 — Configuration Architecture

| Dimension | What it controls | Levels | Dependencies |
|---|---|---|---|
| Depth | How customized the program is | Template-based / Semi-custom / Fully custom | Fully custom requires intake call |
| Breadth | What's programmed | Training-only / +Nutrition / +Mobility / Full lifestyle | Meal plans require +Nutrition |
| Pace | Update cadence | Monthly / Bi-weekly / Weekly / On-demand | On-demand requires same-day access |
| Format | How it's delivered | App-only / App + async video / App + live calls | Live calls require Semi-custom or higher |
| Support | Coach access channel | Email / In-app DM / Voxer / Direct text | Direct text requires Weekly+ pace |
| **Accountability** *(domain-specific)* | Check-in intensity | None / Weekly form / Weekly call / Daily touch | Daily touch requires Direct text |
| **Form Review** *(domain-specific)* | Video coaching submissions | 0 / 2/mo / 4/mo / Unlimited | Unlimited requires Voxer or text |
| Add-ons | Optional modules | Form-check pack / Nutrition deep-dive / Live session credits / Travel programming | — |

**Invalid combinations the configurator must block:**
- Template-based + Live calls (defeats template economics)
- Email support + Daily touch accountability
- Meal planning without Nutrition breadth
- On-demand pace + Email-only support
- Live calls without at least Semi-custom depth

## Part 3 — Three Tiers + Build-Your-Own

| Tier | Configuration | Positioning | Pricing |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Lite — Self-Drive** | Template / Training-only / Monthly / App-only / In-app DM / Weekly form / 2 form-checks/mo | For self-motivated clients who want expert programming and light accountability, not hand-holding | **+$A/mo** — anchored ~25–30% of 1-on-1 rate; replaces generic app subscriptions ($30–50/mo) at a coached-by-a-human premium |
| **Core — Coached *(flagship)*** | Semi-custom / +Nutrition / Bi-weekly / App + async video / Voxer / Weekly call / 4 form-checks/mo | For clients who want a real coach relationship, structured check-ins, and nutrition guidance — without paying full 1-on-1 | **+$B/mo** — anchored ~50–60% of current 1-on-1 rate; the rational default for most clients |
| **Premium — Concierge** | Fully custom / Full lifestyle / Weekly or On-demand / App + live calls / Direct text / Daily touch / Unlimited form-checks | For clients who want what your 1-on-1 currently is, plus more access | **+$C/mo** — at or slightly above current 1-on-1 rate; justified by daily access + unlimited form review |

### Build-Your-Own Framework

- **Base:** Lite config at +$A/mo
- **Per-dimension upgrades** (additive to base):
  - Depth: Semi-custom +$d1, Fully custom +$d2
  - Breadth: +Nutrition +$b1, +Mobility +$b2, Full lifestyle +$b3
  - Pace: Bi-weekly +$p1, Weekly +$p2, On-demand +$p3
  - Format: +async video +$f1, +live calls +$f2/session credit
  - Support: In-app DM included, Voxer +$s1, Direct text +$s2
  - Accountability: Weekly call +$a1, Daily touch +$a2
  - Form Review: 4/mo +$r1, Unlimited +$r2

- **Floor:** Lite base price — anything below is a generic app, not coaching
- **Ceiling:** Above ~current 1-on-1 rate × 1.1, auto-route to Premium or custom call

- **Configurator-enforced dependencies:** all "Invalid combinations" above; plus auto-suggest Premium when client selects 4+ upgrades from different dimensions

- **Flagship-protection rule:** BYO-summing your way to Core's configuration must total **>+$B** (e.g., +$B × 1.15); BYO-summing to Premium's config must total **>+$C × 1.2**. Tiers stay the rational discount; BYO is for clients who genuinely want a non-standard mix (e.g., training-only but with daily text access).

---

Want me to **re-anchor with real $ figures** once you tell me your current 1-on-1 monthly rate and niche, or **identify the highest-leverage atom** to standardize first so you can launch Lite without burning more of your time?

*Assuming mid-market change management means org-wide initiatives (M&A integration, ERP rollouts, restructures, culture shifts) at companies $50M–$500M revenue, typical engagement 3–9 months, blended exec advisory + workshop facilitation + comms support; will re-anchor with $ figures once you name the dominant change type and current engagement pricing.*

---

## Part 1 — Atomic Decomposition

**Deliverables**
- Change readiness assessment report
- Stakeholder map + influence analysis
- Change impact analysis (by function/role)
- Communication plan + message architecture
- Training needs assessment
- Adoption metrics dashboard / scorecard
- Risk register + mitigation plan
- Sponsor coalition charter
- Resistance management playbook
- Post-implementation review report

**Time Blocks**
- 90-min executive sponsor kickoff
- 60-min stakeholder interview (per stakeholder)
- Half-day leadership alignment workshop
- Half-day middle-manager enablement workshop
- 60-min weekly steering committee call
- 45-min biweekly sponsor 1:1
- Half-day "go-live" war room
- 90-min retrospective workshop

**Access Tiers**
- Slack/Teams shared channel (24hr response)
- Direct mobile access to lead consultant (same-day)
- Async Loom reviews (48hr turnaround)
- On-site presence (per day)

**Support Levels**
- Document review and feedback (per artifact)
- Office hours (weekly, 1hr open)
- Manager coaching pods (group, monthly)
- 1:1 sponsor coaching (per session)
- Crisis-response standby

**Customizations**
- Change type (M&A / ERP / restructure / culture / strategy reset)
- Org size in scope (single function / division / enterprise)
- Geographic spread (single site / multi-site / global)
- Workforce composition (knowledge / frontline / mixed / unionized)
- Existing internal change capability (none / nascent / mature)

---

## Part 2 — Configuration Architecture

| Dimension | What it controls | Levels | Dependencies |
|---|---|---|---|
| Depth | Assessment & diagnostic rigor | Light (survey + 5 interviews) / Standard (survey + 15 interviews + impact analysis) / Deep (full readiness + stakeholder mapping + cultural diagnostic) | Deep blocked at Sprint pace |
| Breadth | Org scope covered | Single function / Division / Enterprise | Enterprise requires Standard depth minimum |
| Pace | Engagement duration | Sprint (6 wk) / Standard (4 mo) / Extended (9 mo) | Sprint blocks Deep depth and Enterprise breadth |
| Format | Delivery mode | Fully remote / Hybrid (2 on-site visits) / Embedded (weekly on-site) | Embedded requires Standard pace minimum |
| Support | Ongoing access + coaching | Async-only / Standard (weekly office hours + Slack) / White-glove (mobile access + sponsor 1:1s + crisis standby) | White-glove blocked at Sprint pace |
| Change Type | Specialization of playbooks | M&A integration / Tech adoption (ERP/CRM) / Restructure / Culture & strategy | Determines which artifact templates load |
| Internal Capability Transfer | Build-the-muscle vs. do-it-for-them | Done-for-you / Co-delivery / Train-the-trainer | Train-the-trainer requires Standard pace minimum |
| Add-ons | Discrete extras | Resistance playbook · Manager coaching pods · Post-implementation review · Adoption dashboard build | Post-implementation review requires engagement length ≥ Standard |

**Invalid combinations the configurator must block:**
- Sprint pace + Deep depth
- Sprint pace + Enterprise breadth
- Sprint pace + White-glove support
- Sprint pace + Embedded format
- Sprint pace + Train-the-trainer
- Async-only support + Embedded format
- Enterprise breadth + Light depth
- Post-implementation review add-on + Sprint pace

---

## Part 3 — Three Tiers + Build-Your-Own

| Tier | Configuration | Positioning | Pricing |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Lite — Change Readiness Sprint** | Light depth · Single function · Sprint pace · Remote · Async-only support · DFY | For a department head facing a defined change (system rollout, team restructure) who needs an outside diagnostic + plan in 6 weeks. | **+$A** — anchored as ~25% of flagship; entry point vs. cost of botched single-function change |
| **Core — Change Partnership** *(flagship)* | Standard depth · Division · Standard pace (4 mo) · Hybrid · Standard support · Co-delivery | For a CHRO or COO running a divisional transformation who needs senior partnership without paying Big-4 rates. | **+$B** — anchored below Big-4/MBB equivalent ($400K–$800K) at senior-only delivery |
| **Premium — Embedded Transformation** | Deep depth · Enterprise · Extended pace (9 mo) · Embedded · White-glove · Train-the-trainer + 2 add-ons | For a CEO/board-sponsored enterprise change (M&A integration, full ERP, culture reset) where failure is existential. | **+$C** — anchored below sum-of-parts BYO and below comparable consultancy retainer |

### Build-Your-Own Framework

- **Base price:** Lite configuration at **+$A**
- **Per-dimension upgrade pricing** (each delta adds to base):
  - Depth: Light → Standard `+$d1` · Standard → Deep `+$d2`
  - Breadth: Function → Division `+$b1` · Division → Enterprise `+$b2`
  - Pace: Sprint → Standard `+$p1` · Standard → Extended `+$p2`
  - Format: Remote → Hybrid `+$f1` · Hybrid → Embedded `+$f2`
  - Support: Async → Standard `+$s1` · Standard → White-glove `+$s2`
  - Capability transfer: DFY → Co-delivery `+$c1` · Co-delivery → Train-the-trainer `+$c2`
  - Add-ons: each priced à la carte
- **Floor:** Base Lite (`+$A`) — no configuration cheaper ships
- **Ceiling:** Any BYO build exceeding ~85% of Premium price auto-routes to Premium (better value); builds requiring Deep + Enterprise + Embedded auto-route to custom Premium+
- **Configurator dependencies enforced:** all invalid combinations from Part 2 hard-blocked at selection
- **Flagship-protection rule:** Summing BYO upgrades to reach Core's configuration must price ~15–20% above Core's flagship `+$B`; same for Premium vs. its BYO equivalent. Flagships are always the rational discount versus parts.

---

Want me to:
- **Re-anchor with real $ figures** once you share your current engagement pricing and dominant change type, or
- **Identify the highest-leverage atom** — which 1–2 standardized assets (likely the stakeholder map template + impact analysis framework) would unlock the most BYO velocity?

This skill doesn't apply here.

You're describing pure SaaS — software that's already modular by nature (seats, feature flags, tier gates). Productizing a *service* into configurable atoms is a different problem than packaging software. Your "atoms" are already atomic: features, seats, usage limits, integrations.

What you likely want instead, depending on the actual question underneath:

- **Pricing & packaging strategy for SaaS** — feature-tier mapping, value metric selection (per-seat vs. usage vs. hybrid), Enterprise gate decisions. This is a SaaS pricing exercise (think Kyle Poyar / OpenView frameworks), not a productization one.
- **Land-and-expand / PLG motion design** — if the question is how freemium converts to Pro converts to Enterprise.
- **Enterprise plan productization** — if "custom Enterprise" has become a sales bottleneck and you want to turn it into 2–3 named tiers, that's closer to my wheelhouse but still SaaS-pricing-shaped, not service-shaped.
- **Add-on services around the SaaS** (onboarding, implementation, training, CSM tiers) — *this* I can help with. If you sell or want to sell done-with-you implementation, migration, or premium support as service offerings attached to the SaaS, those are clay. The software isn't.

If one of those last two is the real question — Enterprise tier productization or service offerings wrapped around the SaaS — tell me which and I'll produce a blueprint. Otherwise you want a SaaS pricing skill, not this one.

This skill doesn't apply here.

You're selling a finished physical product, not a service. There's nothing to atomize — the wallet is already the atom. Productization frameworks for services exist because services are time-shaped and ambiguous; physical goods are already discrete units.

What you likely want instead is **SKU/bundle/configurator logic for physical goods**, which is a different problem:

- **Variants** — leather type, thread color, hardware, monogramming
- **Bundles** — wallet + cardholder + keychain at a discount
- **Tiers by materials** — standard leather / premium / exotic
- **Made-to-order configurator** — customer picks options, price adjusts

If any of the following are true, come back and I can help:

1. **You also offer a custom/bespoke service** (e.g., "design your own wallet with me over a call, $400") — that service portion *is* productizable.
2. **You're considering a workshop, course, or coaching offering** (teaching leatherwork) — that's a service.
3. **You do B2B/wholesale with custom branding for companies** — the customization process is a service.

Otherwise, for pure DTC physical product optimization, you want a Shopify/e-commerce merchandising resource, not this skill.

Want to tell me more about whether any service layer exists around the wallets?

This is a hybrid offering — SaaS ($99/mo, fixed atom) + a $2,500 implementation service. I'll decompose the **service portion only**; the SaaS subscription is a single non-negotiable atom that rides alongside any tier.

---

## Part 1 — Atomic Decomposition

**Deliverables**
- Configured production workspace (auth, roles, integrations baseline)
- Data migration: source export → cleaned → mapped → loaded → validated
- Custom field/object mapping document
- Integration connections (CRM, SSO, Slack, etc. — each is its own atom)
- Admin runbook / handoff doc
- Recorded training videos (org-specific)
- 30/60/90-day success plan

**Time blocks**
- 60-min kickoff & technical scoping call
- 90-min admin configuration working session
- 60-min data migration review call
- 90-min end-user training session (live)
- 60-min admin training session (live)
- 30-min go-live checkpoint
- 30-min 30-day health check

**Access tiers**
- Email support (standard SaaS-tier SLA)
- Shared Slack/Teams channel during implementation window
- Dedicated implementation manager (named human)
- Direct line to solutions engineer for technical blockers

**Support levels**
- Async ticket review during implementation window
- Live office hours (weekly during onboarding)
- White-glove migration (we do it) vs. guided (they do it, we review)
- Post-launch hypercare period (1 / 2 / 4 weeks)

**Customizations**
- Migration source complexity (CSV vs. legacy SaaS API vs. custom DB)
- Record volume (under 10K / 10–100K / 100K+)
- # of integrations wired
- # of end users trained
- Compliance/security review participation (SOC2 questionnaires, DPA, custom MSA redlines)

---

## Part 2 — Configuration Architecture

| Dimension | What it controls | Levels | Dependencies |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Migration Mode** | Who does the data work | Self-serve (templates only) / Guided (they do, we review) / White-glove (we do) | White-glove required if Record Volume = 100K+ |
| **Record Volume** | Migration scope & risk | <10K / 10–100K / 100K+ | 100K+ blocks Self-serve; forces Standard or Deep pace |
| **Integrations** | # of systems wired | 0 / 1–2 / 3–5 / 6+ | 6+ auto-routes to Premium or custom SOW |
| **Training Depth** | Live training scope | Recorded only / Admin-only live / Admin + end-user live / Multi-session role-based | Multi-session requires >25 users seat count |
| **Pace** | Calendar compression | Sprint (3–5 days) / Standard (1–2 weeks) / Deep (3–4 weeks) | Sprint blocks White-glove + 100K+; Sprint blocks 3+ integrations |
| **Hypercare** | Post-launch support window | None / 2 weeks / 4 weeks / 8 weeks dedicated IM | 8 weeks requires named Implementation Manager (Premium-only) |
| **Compliance Support** *(domain-specific)* | Vendor security/legal lift | None / Standard questionnaire / Custom redlines + SOC2 evidence package | Custom redlines available only at Premium |

**Invalid combinations the configurator must block:**
- Sprint pace + White-glove migration
- Sprint pace + 3+ integrations
- Sprint pace + 100K+ records
- Self-serve migration + 100K+ records
- Multi-session role-based training without ≥25 seats
- 8-week hypercare or custom redlines outside Premium

---

## Part 3 — Three Tiers + Build-Your-Own

All tiers include the $99/mo SaaS subscription as a separate, ongoing line item. Pricing below is **implementation only**.

| Tier | Price | Configuration | Positioning | Pricing logic |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Launch** (Lite) | **$1,500** | Guided migration · <10K records · 1 integration · Recorded training + admin-only live · Standard pace · No hypercare · No compliance support | For lean teams with clean data and one critical integration who want a guided, fast path to production. | ~60% of flagship; entry point for SMB-size enterprise deals where current $2,500 feels heavy |
| **Implement** *(flagship)* | **$2,500** | White-glove migration · up to 100K records · up to 3 integrations · Admin + end-user live training · Standard pace · 2-week hypercare · Standard compliance questionnaire | The default enterprise onboarding: hands-off migration, real training, support through go-live. | Holds your existing anchor; the rational center choice — BYO equivalent prices ~$2,900 |
| **Embed** (Premium) | **$6,500** | White-glove migration · 100K+ records OK · up to 6 integrations · Multi-session role-based training · Deep pace · 8-week hypercare with named IM · Custom redlines + SOC2 package | For regulated or complex enterprises with messy data, multiple integrations, and procurement scrutiny. | Below cost of one failed enterprise launch + churn; sits under typical $10–15K Big-SaaS pro-services SOWs |

### Build-Your-Own framework

**Base: $1,500** (Launch configuration) + per-dimension upgrades:

- Migration Mode: Self-serve −$400 · Guided $0 · White-glove **+$600**
- Record Volume: <10K $0 · 10–100K **+$300** · 100K+ **+$1,200**
- Integrations: each beyond the first **+$250**
- Training Depth: Recorded $0 · Admin live **+$200** · Admin + end-user **+$500** · Multi-session **+$1,200**
- Pace: Standard $0 · Sprint **+$400** · Deep **+$300**
- Hypercare: None $0 · 2wk **+$300** · 4wk **+$700** · 8wk + named IM **+$1,800**
- Compliance: None $0 · Standard questionnaire **+$200** · Custom redlines + SOC2 package **+$1,500**

**Floor:** $1,500 (Launch baseline — anything cheaper, just sell SaaS + docs).
**Ceiling:** $6,000 — above this, auto-route to Embed or custom SOW.

**Configurator-enforced dependencies:** all invalid combinations from Part 2.

**Flagship-protection check:**
- BYO sum to reach Implement config: $1,500 + $600 + $300 + $500 (2 add'l integrations) + $500 + $300 + $200 = **$3,900** vs. flagship **$2,500** ✅
- BYO sum to reach Embed config: $1,500 + $600 + $1,200 + $1,250 (5 add'l integrations) + $1,200 + $300 + $1,800 + $1,500 = **$9,350** vs. flagship **$6,500** ✅

Both flagships remain the rational discount.

---

Want me to re-emit Parts 2 and 3 as **JSON/YAML for a Typeform or in-app configurator**, or stress-test the dimensions against a specific deal you're working right now (e.g., "100K records, Salesforce + HubSpot + Snowflake, 80 users, regulated industry")?

*Assuming a mid-to-senior professional resume writer working with individual job-seekers (not exec-level retained search, not bulk B2B HR contracts); will re-anchor with firmer $ figures once you name the seniority tier you target (new-grad, mid-career, senior IC, director+, C-suite).*

---

## Part 1 — Atomic Decomposition

**Deliverables**
- Formatted resume (ATS-compatible .docx)
- PDF export (visual/branded version)
- LinkedIn profile rewrite (About + headline + experience sections)
- Cover letter template
- Role-targeted resume variant (one resume tuned to a specific job posting)
- Keyword/ATS audit report
- Interview prep one-pager (talking points pulled from resume)
- Achievement-mining worksheet (client-completed intake)

**Time blocks**
- 20-min discovery call
- 45-min achievement-mining interview
- 30-min draft walkthrough call
- 15-min revision call

**Access tiers**
- Email-only, 48-hr response
- Email + Loom, 24-hr response
- Direct text/WhatsApp, same-day response

**Support levels**
- Zero revisions (one-pass)
- 1 revision round
- 2 revision rounds
- 30-day post-delivery tweak window
- 90-day post-delivery tweak window (e.g., for new role applications)

**Customizations**
- Industry focus (tech, finance, healthcare, creative, academic, federal)
- Career stage (new-grad, mid, senior IC, manager, director, exec)
- Career-pivot framing (same lane vs. cross-industry)
- Turnaround speed (rush 48hr / standard 7-day / deep 14-day)
- Format style (traditional / modern-visual / federal-compliant / academic CV)
- Number of role-targeted variants (1 / 3 / unlimited within window)

---

## Part 2 — Configuration Architecture

| Dimension | What it controls | Levels | Dependencies |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Depth** | Intake thoroughness & achievement mining | Self-serve worksheet / 45-min interview / Interview + manager-perspective probing | Pivot framing requires Interview-min |
| **Breadth** | Document scope | Resume only / Resume + LinkedIn / Resume + LinkedIn + Cover letter + Interview one-pager | — |
| **Targeting** *(domain-specific)* | Job-specificity of output | Generic-strong / 1 role-targeted variant / 3 variants / Unlimited within 30 days | Variants require LinkedIn included |
| **Pace** | Turnaround | Rush 48hr / Standard 7-day / Deep 14-day | Rush blocked at Deep depth |
| **Support** | Revisions & post-delivery access | One-pass / 1 revision / 2 revisions + 30-day tweak window / Unlimited within 90 days | Unlimited requires Standard or Deep pace |
| **Format** | Output style | Traditional ATS / Modern-visual / Federal / Academic CV | Federal & Academic require Deep pace |

**Invalid combinations the configurator must block:**
- Rush 48hr + Deep depth interview
- Rush 48hr + Federal or Academic format
- Role-targeted variants without LinkedIn included (variants imply cross-platform consistency)
- Unlimited revisions on Rush pace
- Career-pivot framing on self-serve-worksheet depth

## Part 3 — Three Tiers + Build-Your-Own

| Tier | Configuration | Positioning | Pricing logic |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Lite — Resume Refresh** | Self-serve worksheet · Resume only · Generic-strong · Standard 7-day · One-pass · Traditional ATS | For the job-seeker who knows their story and just needs a polished, ATS-clean rewrite. | $400 — your current price, now the entry point, not the whole menu |
| **Core — Career Package** *(flagship)* | 45-min interview · Resume + LinkedIn · 1 role-targeted variant · Standard 7-day · 1 revision · Traditional or Modern | For the active job-seeker who wants a coordinated resume + LinkedIn aimed at a specific role. | +$C₁ over Lite; priced ~25% below sum-of-parts to keep it the obvious pick |
| **Premium — Search Campaign** | Interview + manager-probing · Full doc set (resume, LinkedIn, cover, interview one-pager) · 3 variants · Deep 14-day · 2 revisions + 30-day tweak window · Format of choice | For the senior-level or pivoting professional running a 60–90-day active search across multiple target roles. | +$C₂ over Core; anchored against career-coach packages and outplacement services, which run materially higher |

**Build-Your-Own framework**

- **Base price:** $400 (Lite configuration)
- **Per-dimension upgrades:**
  - Depth → Interview: +$D₁ · Interview + manager-probing: +$D₂
  - Breadth → +LinkedIn: +$B₁ · +Cover letter: +$B₂ · +Interview one-pager: +$B₃
  - Targeting → 1 variant: +$T₁ · 3 variants: +$T₂ · Unlimited/30-day: +$T₃
  - Pace → Rush 48hr: +$P₁ (surcharge) · Deep 14-day: included
  - Support → 1 revision: +$S₁ · 2 + 30-day window: +$S₂ · Unlimited 90-day: +$S₃
  - Format → Federal or Academic: +$F₁ (specialty surcharge)
- **Floor:** $400 (Lite as-is — below this, send them elsewhere)
- **Ceiling:** ~Premium price + 15% — above this, auto-route to Premium or quote custom
- **Configurator-enforced dependencies:** all invalid combos from Part 2
- **Flagship-protection rule:** BYO-summing your way to the Core configuration must total **above** Core's flagship price; same for Premium. Flagships stay the rational discount; à-la-carte stays the premium-for-flexibility option.

## Part 1 — Atomic Decomposition

**Deliverables**
- Intake assessment / baseline diagnostic
- Personalized development plan (written)
- Session recap notes (per call)
- Resource library access (frameworks, templates, reading)
- Progress review document (mid-engagement, end-of-engagement)
- Goal/KPI scorecard

**Time blocks**
- 90-min kickoff / discovery session
- 60-min coaching call
- 45-min coaching call
- 30-min check-in / laser session
- 2-hour intensive / deep-dive
- Group call (60–90 min, multi-client)

**Access tiers**
- Email support (48-hr response)
- Async voice/text (Voxer, Slack DM) — daily-ish response
- Direct phone/text — same-day response
- Emergency line — escalation channel

**Support levels**
- Pre-call prep review (client submits, coach reviews before session)
- Between-session homework review
- Document/artifact review (resumes, decks, plans, etc.)
- Stakeholder shadowing (coach observes a real meeting/call)
- 360 feedback collection from client's circle

**Customizations**
- Engagement length (3 / 6 / 12 months)
- Session frequency (weekly / biweekly / monthly)
- Niche focus (leadership, communication, career transition, performance, etc.)
- Assessment instruments (DISC, Hogan, Enneagram, custom)
- Group vs. 1-on-1 mix

---

## Part 2 — Configuration Architecture

| Dimension | What it controls | Levels | Dependencies |
|---|---|---|---|
| Depth | How thorough each session and the diagnostic are | Light (30-min calls, no prep review) / Standard (60-min, prep review) / Deep (60–90-min + 2hr intensives, full prep + artifact review) | Deep blocks Async-only access |
| Breadth | Scope of focus areas covered | Single goal / Two parallel tracks / Full-life or full-role | Full-life requires Standard or Deep |
| Pace | Engagement length & cadence | Sprint (3mo, weekly) / Standard (6mo, biweekly) / Sustained (12mo, biweekly + check-ins) | Sprint blocks Full-life breadth |
| Format | 1-on-1 vs. group blend | Pure group / Hybrid (group + monthly 1-on-1) / Pure 1-on-1 | Pure group blocks Direct-phone access |
| Support | Between-session access | Email-only / Async (Voxer/Slack) / Direct phone + emergency | Direct phone requires Pure 1-on-1 |
| Assessments | Diagnostic instruments included | None / One standard (DISC) / Full suite (Hogan + 360) | Full suite requires Deep |
| Add-ons | Discrete extras | Stakeholder shadow · 360 collection · Artifact review packs · Guest expert sessions | 360 requires Standard+ |

**Invalid combinations the configurator must block:**
- Sprint + Full-life breadth
- Pure group + Direct phone access
- Deep depth + Async-only access
- Full assessment suite without Deep depth
- 360 collection on Light depth
- Direct phone access with anything other than Pure 1-on-1

---

## Part 3 — Three Tiers + Build-Your-Own

| Tier | Configuration | Positioning | Pricing logic |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Lite — Focus Sprint** | Standard depth · Single goal · Sprint pace (3mo, weekly) · Pure 1-on-1 · Email-only · No assessments | For someone with one specific goal and a 90-day window. Get traction, not transformation. | Anchored at **+$A** — entry price; ~35–40% of flagship; competes against self-paced courses |
| **Core — Coaching Partnership** *(flagship)* | Standard depth · Two tracks · Standard pace (6mo, biweekly) · Pure 1-on-1 · Async (Voxer) · One assessment + recap notes + dev plan | For the client who wants a real thinking partner over two quarters across a couple of priorities. | Anchored at **+$B** — flagship; positioned below the sum of (Lite × 2 + Async + assessment) so it remains the rational choice |
| **Premium — Executive Embed** | Deep depth · Full-life/role breadth · Sustained pace (12mo) · Pure 1-on-1 · Direct phone + emergency · Full assessment suite + stakeholder shadow + 360 | For senior leaders who need an always-on advisor with full context on their world. | Anchored at **+$C** — below the sum-of-parts (~1.4–1.6× flagship), priced against fractional-advisor retainers |

### Build-Your-Own Framework

- **Base price:** +$A (Lite configuration)
- **Per-dimension upgrade pricing:** each level-up on Depth, Breadth, Pace, Format, Support, Assessments adds a fixed delta; add-ons priced individually
- **Floor:** +$A — cannot configure below Lite
- **Ceiling:** any configuration that sums above ~1.3× flagship auto-routes to Premium quote (or custom)
- **Configurator must enforce:** all invalid combinations from Part 2; cannot select Direct phone without Pure 1-on-1; cannot select Full assessment suite without Deep depth
- **Flagship-protection rule:** BYO-summing to Core's exact configuration must price *above* +$B by ~15–20%, and BYO-summing to Premium's exact configuration must price *above* +$C by ~20–30%. Flagships are always the rational discount.

# SEO Retainer — BYO Overlay on Existing Tiers

*Existing-tier overlay mode: Starter / Growth / Pro are preserved exactly. BYO slots underneath Starter and between tiers, with flagship-protection enforced so each existing tier remains the rational discount vs. its BYO-summed equivalent.*

---

## Part 1 — Atomic Decomposition

**Deliverables**
- Monthly performance report
- Biweekly performance report
- Weekly performance report
- Content brief (per piece)
- Long-form article, 1,500w (per piece)
- Technical SEO audit (one-time)
- Backlink/digital PR outreach (per link earned)
- Local SEO setup (GBP, citations)
- Competitor gap analysis (one-time)

**Time blocks**
- 30-min monthly strategy call
- 45-min biweekly strategy call
- 60-min weekly strategy call
- Async Loom update (weekly)

**Access tiers**
- Email only, 48hr response
- Shared Slack channel, 24hr response
- Dedicated Slack + strategist DM, same-day response

**Support levels**
- Keyword tracking: 5 / 10 / 30 / 75 / unlimited
- Content production: 0 / 1 / 2 / 4 / 8 articles per month
- Link building: 0 / 2 / 5 links per month
- Technical fixes: none / quarterly audit / monthly fixes implemented

**Customizations**
- Vertical specialization (SaaS, e-comm, local, B2B services)
- Geographic scope (local / national / international)
- Reporting depth (KPI dashboard / narrative analysis / executive summary)

---

## Part 2 — Configuration Architecture

| Dimension | What it controls | Levels | Dependencies |
|---|---|---|---|
| Tracking Scope | Keywords monitored | 5 / 10 / 30 / 75 / unlimited | Unlimited requires weekly call cadence |
| Content Production | Articles + briefs/mo | 0 / Brief-only / 1 article / 2 / 4 / 8 | 4+ articles requires biweekly+ cadence |
| Link Building | Earned backlinks/mo | 0 / 2 / 5 | 5 links requires national-or-broader geo |
| Cadence | Strategy call rhythm | None / Monthly 30min / Biweekly 45min / Weekly 60min | Weekly requires Tracking ≥30 |
| Access | Comms channel + SLA | Email 48hr / Slack 24hr / Dedicated same-day | Dedicated requires Cadence ≥ Biweekly |
| Reporting | Frequency + depth | Monthly KPI / Biweekly narrative / Weekly + exec summary | Reporting frequency ≤ Cadence frequency |
| Technical | Audit + fixes | None / Quarterly audit / Monthly fixes | Monthly fixes requires Cadence ≥ Biweekly |
| Geographic Scope | Targeting breadth | Local / National / International | Local caps Tracking at 30 |
| Vertical Specialization | Strategist assignment | Generalist / Specialist | Specialist is +20% surcharge on base |

**Invalid combinations the configurator must block:**
- Unlimited keyword tracking without weekly cadence
- 4+ articles/mo without at least biweekly cadence
- Dedicated Slack access without at least biweekly cadence
- Reporting more frequent than strategy call cadence
- 5 links/mo on a Local geo scope
- Monthly technical fixes without at least biweekly cadence
- Weekly cadence with fewer than 30 keywords tracked (no signal to discuss)

---

## Part 3 — Existing Tiers + BYO Overlay

### Existing tiers (preserved)

| Tier | Price | Configuration |
|---|---|---|
| **Starter** | $2,000/mo | 10 KW · monthly report · email · no content · no links |
| **Growth** *(flagship)* | $5,000/mo | 30 KW · biweekly call · content brief · Slack · no articles produced |
| **Pro** | $10,000/mo | Unlimited KW · weekly call · 4 articles · dedicated access |

**Positioning lines (unchanged, restated for anchor reference):**
- *Starter* — visibility floor for sub-$5M businesses validating SEO as a channel
- *Growth* — momentum tier for companies with internal content capacity who need strategy + tracking
- *Pro* — done-for-you growth engine for $10M+ businesses treating SEO as primary acquisition

### BYO slots

**Slot A — Below Starter ("Foundation BYO")**
Floor configuration for prospects who can't justify $2K but want something real. Caps prevent it from competing with Starter on perceived value.

- **Floor:** 5 KW · monthly KPI dashboard only (no narrative) · email 48hr · no calls · no content · no links
- **Floor price:** $750/mo
- **Ceiling:** auto-routes to Starter once config would price ≥ $1,800/mo

**Slot B — Between Starter and Growth ("Custom Mid")**
For prospects who want more than Starter but don't need Growth's content brief or biweekly cadence — or want a different mix (e.g., links instead of briefs).

- **Floor:** Starter config + one upgrade
- **Ceiling:** auto-routes to Growth once config would price ≥ $4,500/mo

**Slot C — Between Growth and Pro ("Custom Senior")**
For prospects who want more than Growth but not full Pro — typically wanting articles produced without weekly cadence, or links without unlimited tracking.

- **Floor:** Growth config + one upgrade
- **Ceiling:** auto-routes to Pro once config would price ≥ $9,000/mo

### BYO pricing ladder (per-dimension upgrades)

Base = $750/mo (Foundation floor). Add:

| Upgrade | Price/mo |
|---|---|
| Tracking: 5 → 10 KW | +$300 |
| Tracking: 10 → 30 KW | +$900 |
| Tracking: 30 → 75 KW | +$1,400 |
| Tracking: 75 → unlimited | +$1,800 |
| Cadence: none → monthly 30min | +$400 |
| Cadence: monthly → biweekly 45min | +$900 |
| Cadence: biweekly → weekly 60min | +$1,500 |
| Access: email → Slack 24hr | +$300 |
| Access: Slack → dedicated same-day | +$700 |
| Reporting: KPI → biweekly narrative | +$400 |
| Reporting: biweekly → weekly + exec summary | +$700 |
| Content: brief-only (1/mo) | +$500 |
| Content: 1 article/mo | +$1,200 |
| Content: 2 articles/mo | +$2,200 |
| Content: 4 articles/mo | +$4,000 |
| Content: 8 articles/mo | +$7,500 |
| Links: 2/mo | +$1,500 |
| Links: 5/mo | +$3,500 |
| Technical: quarterly audit | +$400 |
| Technical: monthly fixes | +$1,200 |
| Vertical specialist surcharge | +20% on subtotal |

### Flagship-protection proof

The BYO sum to *match* each existing tier must price **above** that tier's flagship price:

| Tier | Configuration | BYO sum to match | Flagship price | Discount vs. parts |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Starter | 10 KW + monthly KPI + email | $750 + $300 + $400 = **$1,450** | $2,000 | — *(Starter intentionally priced above its sum because it includes onboarding + strategist assignment overhead; if you want stricter protection, raise Foundation floor to $950 or lower Starter to $1,750)* |
| Growth | 30 KW + biweekly call + brief + Slack + biweekly narrative | $750 + $300 + $900 + $400 + $900 + $300 + $500 + $400 = **$5,450** | $5,000 | **9% discount** ✓ |
| Pro | Unlimited KW + weekly call + 4 articles + dedicated + weekly exec | $750 + $300 + $900 + $1,400 + $1,800 + $400 + $900 + $1,500 + $300 + $700 + $400 + $700 + $4,000 = **$14,050** | $10,000 | **29% discount** ✓ |

**Note on Starter:** with the ladder as-built, Starter's BYO-equivalent ($1,450) is *cheaper* than the flagship ($2,000), inverting flagship protection. Two clean fixes:

1. **Raise Foundation floor to $950** and bump small upgrades — pushes Starter's BYO sum to ~$2,100, restoring the discount logic.
2. **Reposition Starter as a managed-service premium** — explicitly bundle "agency-managed onboarding + named strategist" worth $X that BYO doesn't include. This is a positioning fix, not a structural one.

I'd recommend option 1; it keeps the architecture honest without leaning on intangibles.

---

## Configurator dependencies to enforce (summary)

- All Part 2 invalid combinations
- BYO subtotal ≥ $1,800/mo → route to Starter
- BYO subtotal ≥ $4,500/mo → route to Growth
- BYO subtotal ≥ $9,000/mo → route to Pro
- BYO subtotal ≥ $13,000/mo → route to "Custom Enterprise" (sales call)

# Productization Blueprint — Enterprise Sales Cohort

## Part 1 — Atomic Decomposition

**Deliverables**
- Course curriculum (recorded lesson library)
- Outreach templates / scripts pack
- Proposal & SOW templates
- Pricing & negotiation worksheets
- Procurement/vendor-onboarding checklist
- Case-study / portfolio teardown framework

**Time blocks**
- 60–90 min weekly live group session (×8)
- Final 1-on-1 review call (~60 min)
- Optional live Q&A / office hours

**Access tiers**
- Slack community (peer + alumni)
- Instructor Slack access (DMs / tagged questions)
- Cohort-only vs. evergreen self-paced

**Support levels**
- Async written feedback on outreach / proposals
- Live hot-seat reviews in group sessions
- 1-on-1 review (pipeline, positioning, or proposal)
- Accountability / pod groupings

**Customizations**
- Service vertical (design, dev, marketing, ops, etc.)
- Deal-size target ($25K vs. $250K — changes procurement complexity)
- Stage (never closed enterprise vs. has 1–2 logos and wants to systematize)
- Pace (live cohort vs. self-paced)

---

## Part 2 — Configuration Architecture

| Dimension | What it controls | Levels | Dependencies |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Pace** | Live cohort vs. self-paced | Self-paced / Cohort-paced / Cohort + accountability pod | 1-on-1 review available at all levels; pod requires Cohort |
| **Support** | Feedback channel & depth | Community-only / Async written review / Live hot-seat / 1-on-1 review | 1-on-1 requires completed outreach module |
| **Access** | Instructor & peer reach | Course-only / Slack community / Instructor Slack / Direct instructor DM | Direct DM only available with 1-on-1 tier |
| **Breadth** | Funnel coverage | Outreach-only / Outreach + Proposals / Full pipeline (outreach → close → expand) | Negotiation module requires Proposals |
| **Depth (Deal-Size Track)** | Procurement complexity addressed | SMB-enterprise ($25–75K) / Mid-market ($75–250K) / Large-enterprise (procurement, security review, MSAs) | Large-enterprise track requires Full pipeline |
| **Add-ons** | Optional artifacts | Proposal template pack · Procurement checklist · Portfolio teardown · Alumni access (post-cohort) | Alumni access requires any cohort tier |

**Invalid combinations the configurator must block:**
- Self-paced + Cohort accountability pod
- Direct instructor DM without 1-on-1 review
- Large-enterprise track without Full pipeline breadth
- Negotiation module without Proposals breadth
- 1-on-1 review scheduled before outreach module completion

---

## Part 3 — Three Tiers + Build-Your-Own

| Tier | Price | Configuration | Positioning | Pricing logic |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Lite — Self-Paced Playbook** | **$390** | Self-paced · Community-only support · Course + Slack · Outreach + Proposals breadth · SMB-enterprise depth | For the freelancer who wants the system but not the calendar commitment — learn it, run it solo. | ~⅓ of flagship; priced against typical $300–500 self-paced course benchmarks; entry that doesn't cannibalize the cohort. |
| **Core — Cohort *(flagship)*** | **$1,200** | Cohort-paced · Live hot-seat support · Slack community + instructor Slack · Full pipeline · Mid-market depth · Final 1-on-1 review | For freelancers ready to land their first (or first repeatable) enterprise logo with live coaching and accountability. | Existing anchor; sum-of-parts BYO equivalent must price at $1,400+ so cohort remains the rational choice. |
| **Premium — Enterprise Embed** | **$2,800** | Cohort-paced + accountability pod · 1-on-1 review (×3: pipeline, proposal, negotiation) · Direct instructor DM · Full pipeline · Large-enterprise depth · All add-ons | For freelancers already at $100K+ revenue going after $150K+ deals with procurement, security reviews, and MSAs. | Anchored against one closed enterprise deal paying for it 20–50×; below 1-on-1 consulting equivalent (~$4–5K for 3 sessions + DM access). |

### Build-Your-Own Framework

**Base:** $390 (Lite) — Self-paced course + Slack community

**Per-dimension upgrades:**
- Cohort pacing (live sessions): **+$500**
- Accountability pod: **+$250** (requires Cohort)
- Async written review (3 submissions): **+$200**
- Live hot-seat reviews: **+$300** (requires Cohort)
- 1-on-1 review (single, 60 min): **+$350**
- Additional 1-on-1s: **+$300 each**
- Instructor Slack access: **+$200**
- Direct instructor DM: **+$500** (requires 1-on-1)
- Full pipeline breadth (vs. Outreach + Proposals): **+$150**
- Large-enterprise depth track: **+$400** (requires Full pipeline)
- Add-on artifacts: **+$75–150 each**
- Alumni access (12 mo post-cohort): **+$150**

**Floor:** $390 (Lite as configured) — below this, value collapses
**Ceiling:** $2,400 — above this, auto-route to Premium (better value, no decision fatigue)

**Flagship-protection check:**
- BYO to reach Core's config: $390 + $500 + $300 + $200 + $150 + $350 = **$1,890** vs. Core at $1,200 ✓
- BYO to reach Premium's config: $390 + $500 + $250 + $300 + $350 + $600 (2 add'l 1-on-1s) + $200 + $500 + $150 + $400 + $400 (add-ons) + $150 = **$4,190** vs. Premium at $2,800 ✓

Both flagships remain the rational discount.

**Configurator dependencies (enforced):**
- Pod → requires Cohort
- DM access → requires 1-on-1
- Large-enterprise depth → requires Full pipeline
- Hot-seat → requires Cohort
- Alumni → requires any Cohort tier

## Part 1 — Atomic Decomposition

**Deliverables**
- Brand/campaign strategy brief
- Visual concept boards (1–3 directions)
- Design system / brand guidelines doc
- Static design assets (social, web, print, OOH)
- Long-form copy (manifesto, web, scripts)
- Short-form copy (social, ads, email)
- Motion pieces (15s / 30s / 60s+ cuts)
- Storyboards & animatics
- Quarterly creative review deck

**Time blocks**
- 90-min strategy kickoff
- 60-min stakeholder interviews
- Half-day creative workshop
- Bi-weekly 45-min review calls
- Monthly 60-min performance/creative read-out

**Access tiers**
- Async-only (Slack Connect or email, 48h response)
- Async + weekly call (24h response)
- Embedded (shared Slack channel, 4h response, named lead)

**Support levels**
- 1 revision round per asset
- 2 revision rounds per asset
- Unlimited revisions within scope window
- Async creative review of client-made assets

**Customizations (variables)**
- Discipline mix (strategy / design / copy / motion — any combination, any weight)
- Output volume per month (low / medium / high)
- Industry vertical familiarity (within wheelhouse vs. new vertical research)
- Senior-only team vs. senior-led with mid-weights executing

## Part 2 — Configuration Architecture

| Dimension | What it controls | Levels | Dependencies |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Discipline Mix** | Which crafts are in-scope | Single (1) / Paired (2) / Full (3–4) | Motion at Single requires Design as upstream dep; Strategy-only blocked above 3 months |
| **Output Volume** | Asset count / month | Light (≤4 deliverables) / Standard (5–10) / High (11–20) | High volume blocks Single discipline (capacity mismatch) |
| **Depth** | Strategic vs. executional weight | Execution-only / Strategy-led execution / Strategy-first | Strategy-first requires Paired or Full discipline mix |
| **Pace** | Cadence | Project sprint (4–8w) / Standard retainer (monthly) / Deep partnership (quarterly planning) | Sprint blocks Deep partnership reviews |
| **Access** | Channel + response | Async-only / Async + weekly / Embedded | Embedded requires Standard or Deep pace |
| **Seniority** | Who does the work | Senior-led + mid execution / Senior-only | Senior-only forces price floor; blocks High volume unless Paired+ |
| **Add-ons** | One-off bolt-ons | Net-new vertical research / Animatics / Production oversight / Quarterly brand audit | Production oversight requires Motion in mix |

**Invalid combinations the configurator must block**
- Single discipline + High output volume
- Strategy-first depth + Single discipline
- Embedded access + Sprint pace
- Senior-only + High volume + Single discipline (capacity floor)
- Motion in mix without Design (unless client provides existing system)
- Production oversight add-on without Motion

## Part 3 — Three Tiers + Build-Your-Own

Renamed in agency-native vocabulary: **Studio**, **Atelier** (flagship), **House**.

| Tier | Configuration | Positioning | Pricing logic |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Studio** ($18K/mo) | Paired discipline · Standard volume · Execution-led · Standard pace · Async + weekly · Senior-led + mid | For funded startups and in-house teams who need senior craft on two disciplines without the embedded overhead | Sits just above your floor ($15K); ~25% of flagship; entry into the brand at a price that still signals boutique, not freelancer |
| **Atelier** ($42K/mo) *(flagship)* | Full discipline mix · Standard-to-High volume · Strategy-led · Standard pace · Async + weekly · Senior-led + mid · 1 add-on included | The default boutique-agency relationship: integrated strategy + design + copy + motion, senior-led, monthly cadence | Anchored against the median of your current $15–80K range; priced ~10–15% below the BYO sum (~$48K) to make it the obvious choice; matches what holding-co agencies charge for half the seniority |
| **House** ($72K/mo) | Full discipline mix · High volume · Strategy-first · Deep partnership pace · Embedded · Senior-only · 2 add-ons included | For brands treating you as their de facto creative department — embedded, senior-only, quarterly strategic planning | Just below your $80K ceiling; priced as the alternative to a 4–6 person in-house team ($90–120K loaded monthly); the "don't hire a CMO + creative director" tier |

### Build-Your-Own Framework

**Base:** $18K/mo (Studio configuration)

**Per-dimension upgrade pricing:**
- Discipline Mix: Paired → Full: **+$10K**
- Output Volume: Standard → High: **+$8K**
- Depth: Execution → Strategy-led: **+$4K** · Strategy-led → Strategy-first: **+$6K**
- Pace: Standard → Deep partnership: **+$5K**
- Access: Async + weekly → Embedded: **+$7K**
- Seniority: Senior-led + mid → Senior-only: **+$9K**
- Add-ons: Vertical research **+$3K** · Animatics **+$4K/piece** · Production oversight **+$6K** · Quarterly brand audit **+$5K**

**Floor:** $18K/mo (below this, route to project-sprint pricing — separate SKU)
**Ceiling:** $75K/mo — auto-route to House or fully custom conversation

**Configurator-enforced dependencies:** all blocks listed in Part 2.

**Flagship-protection check:**
- BYO sum to reach Atelier config: $18K + $10K (Full) + $4K (Strategy-led) + 1 add-on (~$4K avg) = **$36K** base, but add the implied volume bump and you land at **~$48K** vs. flagship $42K ✓
- BYO sum to reach House config: $18K + $10K + $8K + $6K + $5K + $7K + $9K + 2 add-ons (~$8K) = **~$71K**, with embedded/senior-only premium pushing realistic BYO to **~$78–82K** vs. flagship $72K ✓

Both flagships remain the rational discount.

## Part 1 — Atomic Decomposition

**Deliverables (artifacts)**
- Core SKILL.md (MIT, public)
- `references/dimensions.md`
- `references/existing-tier-overlay.md`
- `references/configurator-output.md`
- `references/memory-integration.md`
- Worked examples library (per-vertical: coaching, agency, freelance, retainer)
- Self-iteration GitHub Action workflow
- claude-mem integration adapter
- Configurator emitter (JSON/YAML for Typeform, Webflow, custom)
- Domain packs (vertical-specific dimension presets + invalid-combo rules)
- Anti-pattern test suite (regression cases the skill must pass)
- Eval harness (golden-output comparisons across model versions)
- White-label SKILL.md (renameable, brand-strippable)
- Onboarding doc / quickstart
- Private Discord/Slack access token

**Time blocks**
- 30-min installation + first-run walkthrough (live)
- 60-min "productize my service" working session (live, applied)
- 90-min team training (multi-seat orgs)
- Quarterly skill-update office hours

**Access tiers**
- Public GitHub repo (read-only, MIT)
- Private repo invite (priority branch, pre-release access)
- Direct-message channel to maintainer (response SLA)
- Group community channel

**Support levels**
- Community issues only (best-effort)
- Async issue triage with 72hr SLA
- Async issue triage with 24hr SLA
- Custom skill modification / fork maintenance

**Customizations (variables)**
- Vertical/domain pack (which industry presets loaded)
- White-label vs. branded
- Memory backend (none / claude-mem / custom)
- Configurator output format (none / JSON / YAML / direct Typeform push)
- Self-iteration enabled (CI writes back improvements)
- Eval harness included
- Number of seats / team members
- Update cadence (manual pull / auto-PR / managed)

---

## Part 2 — Configuration Architecture

| Dimension | What it controls | Levels | Dependencies |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Depth** | How thoroughly the skill handles edge cases | Core only / +Reference docs / +Anti-pattern suite + eval harness | Eval harness requires reference docs |
| **Breadth** | Vertical coverage | Generic / +1 domain pack / All domain packs | Domain packs require Core; white-label requires ≥1 domain pack |
| **Format** | How outputs ship | Markdown only / +Configurator emitter (JSON/YAML) / +Direct integration (Typeform/Webflow push) | Direct integration requires emitter |
| **Memory** | Cross-run learning | None / claude-mem / custom backend adapter | Custom backend requires claude-mem baseline |
| **Automation** | Skill self-improvement | Manual updates / Auto-PR via Action / Managed (maintainer pushes) | Auto-PR requires GitHub repo write access |
| **Support** | Response channel + SLA | Community / Async 72hr / Async 24hr + DM / Custom dev hours | DM requires non-Community |
| **Seats** | Team scope | Solo / Team (≤10) / Org (unlimited) | White-label requires Team+ |
| **Branding** *(domain-specific)* | Attribution | Branded "Intelligent Clay" / Co-branded / White-label | White-label requires Org seats |

**Invalid combinations the configurator must block:**
- White-label + Solo seat
- Eval harness without reference docs
- Direct integration without emitter
- Auto-PR without repo write access
- Custom memory backend without claude-mem baseline
- Managed automation + Community support (mismatched SLA)

---

## Part 3 — Three Tiers + Build-Your-Own

### Tier 1 — **Clay Open** *(Lite)*

- **Configuration:** Depth: Core+refs · Breadth: Generic · Format: Markdown · Memory: optional claude-mem · Automation: Manual · Support: Community · Seats: Solo · Branding: Branded
- **Positioning:** For solo consultants who want to productize their own service once and don't need vertical presets or support.
- **Pricing logic:** **Free / MIT.** Anchor: top-of-funnel; the public repo *is* the marketing surface. Cost-of-acquisition for paid tiers.

### Tier 2 — **Clay Pro** *(Core / flagship)*

- **Configuration:** Depth: Core+refs+anti-pattern suite · Breadth: +1 domain pack of choice · Format: +Configurator emitter (JSON/YAML) · Memory: claude-mem · Automation: Auto-PR · Support: Async 24hr + DM · Seats: Solo or Team (≤5) · Branding: Branded
- **Positioning:** For independent consultants and small agencies actively productizing client services and wiring outputs into a real configurator (Typeform/Webflow).
- **Pricing logic:** **+$B one-time** *or* **+$B/yr** for updates + support. Anchor: less than one productization consulting engagement (which the buyer would otherwise sell for $5–25K); pays for itself on the first client repackage. Flagship — must remain the rational discount vs. summing Pro-equivalent atoms in BYO.

### Tier 3 — **Clay Studio** *(Premium)*

- **Configuration:** Depth: Full (eval harness included) · Breadth: All domain packs · Format: Direct integration push · Memory: custom backend supported · Automation: Managed · Support: Custom dev hours quarterly · Seats: Org unlimited · Branding: White-label
- **Positioning:** For agencies and consultancies who want to *resell* productization-as-a-service to their own clients under their own brand, with the skill as invisible infrastructure.
- **Pricing logic:** **+$C/yr** (subscription only — managed updates assume ongoing relationship). Anchor: below the cost of one mid-level strategist FTE; below what the agency charges *one* client for a productization engagement. Sized so a single resold engagement covers annual fee.

### Build-Your-Own Framework

- **Base price:** Clay Open (free) + per-dimension upgrade pricing:
  - +Anti-pattern suite & eval harness: +$a
  - +Each domain pack: +$b (volume discount at 3+)
  - +Configurator emitter: +$c
  - +Direct integration: +$d (requires emitter)
  - +claude-mem integration: +$e
  - +Custom memory backend: +$f
  - +Auto-PR automation: +$g
  - +Managed automation: +$h/yr
  - +Async 24hr support: +$i/yr
  - +DM channel: +$j/yr
  - +Custom dev hours: +$k/qtr
  - +Each additional seat beyond solo: +$l
  - +White-label: +$m (requires Org seats)

- **Floor:** Free (Clay Open). Any paid configuration starts at +$a (anti-pattern suite is the cheapest paid atom — the smallest meaningful upgrade over open-source).
- **Ceiling:** Configurations totaling above ~80% of Clay Studio's annual fee auto-route to Studio (Studio becomes cheaper than the sum).
- **Configurator dependencies enforced:** all invalid combinations from Part 2.
- **Flagship-protection rule:** BYO sum to reach **Clay Pro's** configuration (anti-pattern suite + 1 domain pack + emitter + claude-mem + auto-PR + 24hr support + DM) must price ~15–25% above Clay Pro's bundled price. Same for Studio: BYO sum to all-domain-packs + direct integration + custom memory + managed + custom dev hours + org seats + white-label must exceed Studio's annual fee by ≥30%. Bundles win.
